name: Ashvin A. Swaminathan
title: NSF Postdoctoral Fellow & Benjamin Peirce Fellow
researchBlurb: My research lies in number theory and algebraic geometry, with a focus on arithmetic statistics, invariant theory, the Cohen-Lenstra heuristics, rational points on varieties, Diophantine equations, and enumerative geometry.
aiBlurb: I am increasingly focused on building workflows that combine large language models, symbolic computation, and formal verification to attack research problems in number theory.
education: I received my AB in mathematics from Harvard College, with a senior thesis supervised by Joe Harris and Anand Patel, as well as an AM in Physics. I completed my MA and PhD at Princeton under Manjul Bhargava.
currentActivity: In Fall 2025, I was on leave from Harvard, participating as a visiting researcher in the special semester on arithmetic statistics at the new Lodha Mathematical Sciences Institute in Mumbai, India.
personal: Outside of mathematics, I love animals and volunteer at Broken Tail Animal Rescue. If you are interested in adopting an animal, please check out their website!

highlights.text: New preprint: "The existence of infinitely many cubic fields with class group of exact 2-rank 1" (with M. Bhargava, A. Shankar, and A. Siad)
Workshop on Arithmetic Statistics of Algebraic Objects, Mathematisches Forschungsinstitut Oberwolfach, November 2025
Workshop on Arithmetic Statistics, Centre de Recherches Mathématiques, Université de Montréal, March 2026.
Upcoming: BU Number Theory Seminar, April 2026.
